---
# Member parameters
title: Complete title (academic title + name) which appears at the top of the profile page
academic_title: Complete academic title (e.g. Dr., Prof Dr., PhD, MSc.)
name: Full name (first name + surname)
first_name: First name (field currently unused, actually but good to have for future use)
surname: Last name (field used for sorting people alphabetically by surname!)
categories:
  - Name of Faculty. This appears in the "Faculty" sidebar for Fellows and Members

# There is no limit to the number of memberships a person can have. All memberships must have a type, status, and start year.
# See documentation for more details on the different types of memberships and their status options.
memberships:
  - type: scientific board
    status: alumni
    start: 2017
    end: 2019
    notes: Psychology
  - type: management
    status: active
    start: 2017
    position: Managing Director of the OSC
    faculty: Psychology & Education

# if desired by the member, add a photo to the people/people/images folder and link to it here
photo: "images/FIRSTNAME_LASTNAME.jpeg"
email: Email address, ideally LMU official
personal: |
  For users that appear in a section that uses cards with preview descriptions (e.g. Management, Staff, Advisors), this is the short description that appears on the card. It should be a single paragraph of no more than 2-3 sentences.
  
  If this is not filled out, the system will automatically use the first 2-3 sentences of the mission statement as the preview description on the card.

# certain keywords for the label are recognized and the output is sorted in this priority: LMU Profile, Professional Profile, Personal Website, and Professional Website. see documentation for details on these labels. other links appear in the order they are listed in the YAML file.
links:
  - label: "Personal Website"
    url: "https://www.nicebread.de"
  - label: "LMU Profile"
    url: "https://www.lmu.de/psy/de/personen/kontaktseite/felix-schoenbrodt-21fe5d01.html"
  - label: "Department of Psychology"
    url: "https://www.psy.lmu.de/"

# Social media links - variable number
social_media:
  Mastodon: "https://scicomm.xyz/@nicebread"
  GitHub: "https://github.com/nicebread"
  ORCID: "https://orcid.org/0000-0002-8282-3910"

# Mission statement paragraphs - variable number
mission_statement: |
  This is the description that appears on someone's profile page. It can be as long as you like, and include multiple paragraphs.

  Paragraphs are separated by a blank line. In general, Markdown formatting is supported, so you can include links, lists, etc. For example, you could link to your personal website like this: [My Personal Website](https://www.example.com). Or you could include a list of your research interests like this:

    - Thing 1
    - Thing 2
    - Thing 3

  Do note that the indenting of the text in the YAML file is important. The text should be indented with two spaces underneath the mission_statement key, and the pipe symbol (|) should be used to indicate that the text is a multi-line string. This allows for proper formatting when the text is rendered on the profile page. For example, the text here in this example is properly formatted and indented.

# Research interests - variable number
research_interests:
  - "Open Science"
  - "Psychology"
  - "Psychological Methods"
  - "Metascience"

# Publications - variable number
publications:
  - year: 2025
    title: Paper title
    authors: List of authors, likely in the format "LastName Initials, LastName Initials, ..."
    journal: Journal name
    doi_url: DOI link to the publication


# these must be included always.
template: templates/people-template.html
filters: 
    - templates/people_filter.lua
---

<!-- additional content **can** be included here, and appears after the Mission Statement but before the Research Interests. Any content that Quarto can render can be included here, namely R/Python/Julia code chunks, Markdown formatting, more HTML, etc. Main reasons to use this though: code blocks, and to include files. -->
